rationale: Neither candidate hits the ground truth's actual substantive concern (dsocek's naming-conflict/_packed_modules_mapping comment, which appears tied to a part of the PR later removed per tthakkal's reply). X at least raises analogous design concerns — hardcoded component lists vs. single-source-of-truth (_dit_modules), and a silent-no-op risk for text-encoder LoRA that echoes the PR's own stated failure mode — with specific file/line grounding and concrete suggested changes. Y is a well-grounded validation pass (repo-wide greps, exact line citations) but surfaces zero findings or suggestions, making it precise but not useful as a review. X provides more actionable value despite imperfect overlap with the ground truth.
